raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Governor Gavin Newsom's office has launched a smear campaign against USC Professor Michael Mische due to his research on California's energy future.",
    "Senate Minority Leader Jones asserts that Governor Newsom is not addressing Californians' concerns about affordability regarding energy.",
    "Professor Mische is a widely published academic who has authored over 150 papers focused on California's oil and gas sector.",
    "The California Independent System Operator, appointed by Newsom, is capable of analyzing the economics of gasoline supply and demand.",
    "The closure of two in-state refineries would reduce gasoline supply and likely drive up prices.",
    "Professor Mische's previous work with Saudi Arabia focused on diversifying its economy away from fossil fuels and ended in 2022.",
    "Professor Mische has never worked to promote Saudi oil interests or for any Saudi oil company or refinery.",
    "Professor Mische's recent analysis advocates for an increase in in-state oil production and refining to reduce reliance on foreign oil.",
    "Senate Minority Leader Jones warns that losing refineries will destabilize California's fuel supply and significantly increase gas prices.",
    "The closure of refineries could eliminate thousands of jobs and increase California's dependence on out-of-state fuel."
  ]
}
```
